en.extract:
The sombre pigeon (Cryptophaps poecilorrhoa) is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is the only species within the genus Cryptophaps. It is endemic to the Indonesian island of Sulawesi in Wallacea. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

The '''sombre pigeon''' ('''''Cryptophaps poecilorrhoa''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is the only species within the genus '''''Cryptophaps'''''.<ref>{{Cite journal |author=Sergio L. Pereira |author2=Kevin P. Johnson |author3=Dale H. Clayton |author4=Allan J. Baker |name-list-style=amp |year=2007 |title=Mitochondrial and nuclear DNA sequences support a Cretaceous origin of Columbiformes and a dispersal-driven radiation in the Paleogene |journal=[[Systematic Biology]] |volume=56 |issue=4 |pages=656–672 |doi=10.1080/10635150701549672 |pmid=17661233|doi-access=free }}</ref> It is [[endemic]] to the [[Indonesia]]n island of [[Sulawesi]] in [[Wallacea]].<ref name="iucn status 13 November 2021" /> Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.<ref name="iucn status 13 November 2021" />
